slides.title: How to Reduce Screen Time for Kids
slides.body: 5 simple habits that actually work — without daily battles.

slides.title: Create a Before-Screen Routine
slides.body: Before tablets or YouTube, encourage 1–2 simple activities. Small habits create natural transitions.

slides.title: Keep It Simple
slides.body: Skip the charts. Choose 1–2 tasks your child can complete every day. Consistency beats complexity.

slides.title: Make It Predictable
slides.body: When kids know what comes next, they ask less. Predictability reduces negotiations.

slides.title: Celebrate the Habit
slides.body: Instead of 'you earned your tablet,' try 'I'm proud of how you remembered your routine.'

slides.title: Let the Routine Do the Work
slides.body: The best routines become automatic. Less negotiating, more following what they already know.

caption:
Screen time doesn't have to mean daily battles 😤

Here are 5 simple habits that help kids transition into screen time naturally — no arguments, no negotiations.

Save this and try one today 👇
